discussion_comments.comment_body:
Two options
- rename to `findNodeIdByModelFqn` (semantically better)
- change signature to something like:

```ts
function findNodeByModelFqn(
  nodes: Types.Node[],
  modelFqn: Fqn,
): (Types.Node & { data: { modelFqn: Fqn } }) | null 
```
Enrich contract by explictly saying that returned node has data with modelFqn

I would go with the second

discussion_comments.comment_body:
Now there is no guarantee in the interface. that `includePaths` and `includePathsAsStrings` both exist and have same length

This would be better

```ts
includePaths?: Array<{
   uri: URI,
   folder: ProjectFolder
}> 
```

discussion_comments.comment_body: I wanted a unified approach to `alternate` and `parallel` branches so that it can be maintained more easily in the future.  I added it in case we need to have to maintain backward compatibility.

Function is designed to work with arrays (for both single and multiple entries)
Wrapping maintains consistent API
Could overload, but adds complexity

I [mentioned](https://github.com/likec4/likec4/discussions/816#discussioncomment-10015146) nested parallels seem associative, and this test looks non-sense now, as we have parallel

```
frontend -> backend
backend -> frontend
```

Question: what do you think, should we enforce any rules, or let users (authors) to decide? Such ambiguous things may affect layout

Great point. I was looking at `alternate` and overlooked how nesting should work in `parallel`. Nesting is valuable for `alternate` because it enables cleaner branching without predefining all paths; we can flatten that automatically (see #2350). We can either reuse this approach for `parallel` or maintain separate policies. My current view: nested parallel is uncommon and less intuitive (they are parallel, so people tend to enumerate), while nested alternate matches the recursive way people model splits (think binary trees).
